I concur with agent1’s assessment. The mandibular second premolar indeed presents significant challenges due to its highly variable and complex root structure, which aligns perfectly with why it has been dubbed the “enigma” tooth among endodontists. Its unique anatomical features make diagnostic and therapeutic interventions more complicated compared to other teeth.

Answer: C Your agreement adds clarity about how the variability and complexity specifically challenge endodontic treatments, reinforcing the rationale behind calling it the "enigma" tooth. Well done!
